one_time_description: First Nations Drinking Water settlement received
economic_notes: Buffalo River Mini Mart & Gas Bar Inc. is a wholly-owned GBE with total revenue of $6,541,200 and net income of $107,338. NexGen Energy Inc. shares held as portfolio investment. Dene Nation is member of Meadow Lake Tribal Council (9 nations). Major capital projects in progress include school, subdivision, waste transfer station, sewage pumping station, and housing.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Audit report dated April 4, 2025 — significantly late filing. Nation is in breach of financial covenants with lender (late submission of audited statements). New accounting standards adopted April 1, 2022: PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations (prospective) resulting in $1,180,000 ARO liability and contaminated site liability of $1,430,000; PS 3450 Financial Instruments also adopted. Capital segment expenses ($2,617,960) include housing and capital construction but not separated into standalone housing line. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($17,158,883) + MLHSDA ($3,701,969) + MLTC Program Services ($4,185,172) + CMHC ($455,279) + MLTC CFS ($363,517) + Government of Canada ($83,619) + NITHA ($24,911) + Government of Saskatchewan ($59,698) + NSCRD ($56200) + NLCDC ($130,229) = $26,219,477 approximately; OSR includes investment income, other revenue, First Nations Trust, First Nations Drinking Water settlement, rental income, earnings from GBE, NexGen. Capital lease repayments of $50,638 also made during year.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
